headline: A boy passionate about MLLMs
bioMarkdown:
Hi, I'm Junjie Wu. I received my B.Eng. in Computer Science and Technology from Tianjin University (TJU), and I am currently pursuing my M.Eng. in Electronic Information at TJU, advised by Prof. Qilong Wang in the Machine Learning and Data Mining Laboratory.

My research focuses on **multimodal large language models**, **visual representation learning**, and **domain-specific post-training**. During my master's study, I have worked on higher-order visual token representations for Vision Transformers, domain-specific vision-language pre-training, and efficient visual token aggregation and compression for MLLMs.

I have first-author or co-first-author publications at ICLR 2025, ICCV 2025, and ICMR 2026. Since **2024**, I have also been working as an Algorithm Researcher intern at Glority, building domain-specific multimodal data and model pipelines for real-world applications.

Beyond research, I enjoy exploring emerging technologies and staying close to fast-moving ideas. I also love sports, especially basketball (captain of the Tianjin University basketball team, 2021-2023) and tennis.
blogTagline: Notes on multimodal learning, visual representation, and domain-specific AI.
